title: #2 - Back Office setup
bodyHtml:
Setting up your Back Office is one of the most important steps to start selling on Back Market.

Each of the following points is essential for a successful start on the platform and will help you answer the test at the end of this training to increase your current daily sales limit from 1 to 3.
 

 

Products and listings

To understand how our marketplace works, first learn the differences between a product and a listing.

 

  • A product refers to a device with several attributes (model, brand, color, weight, operator, processor, etc.). A product can be used by several sellers on Back Market. It only needs to be created once in the catalog.
  • A listing refers to the offer that a seller makes on this product. It is up to you to create your listing for an existing product.


Learn more about the difference between products and listings

 

Create your listings

In order for your account to be live, create your listings with up-to-date prices and stock amounts. You can add your listings manually, through a CSV file or even connect your API to do it automatically.
Learn how to create listings

 

Create your products

If your product doesn’t already exist in our catalog, or if no similar product exists to duplicate, you need to create the product.
Learn how to create products in the Back Office

 

Avoid mistakes in creating your listings and products

Properly creating or updating your listings and products on our marketplace is key. 

If you encounter any errors during these processes, contact the Seller Support Center immediately as errors can mean a loss of potential sales for you and your business.

 

 

Note that once you have created a product, it needs to be validated by our catalogue team which can take up to 48 hours. 

When choosing your listings’ grades, make sure your grading system is matching Back Market’s standards.

Learn about the physical appearance grades.

 

Shipping methods

In order for your listings to go live once created, you need to add shipping methods for each market. If you don’t add any shipping method, your ads will remain offline. 
Learn more about setting up shipping methods

Your shipping methods need to meet our delivery requirements, especially in terms of delivery times for each category.
Learn more about delivery requirements
 


The BackBox

To sell on Back Market, your listed products need to be visible to customers. To do this, you need to win what we call The BackBox, our in-house algorithm which advertises to customers the best quality-price ratio for each listing on the website. To understand more about the BackBox, please watch the video below. 
Learn the ins and outs of The BackBox
 

 

Our Seller Support Center

Our Seller Support Center (SSC) is your best ally at Back Market. It’s here to answer all your questions and provide you with daily support.
Learn how the Seller Support Center works

The Seller Support Center serves as your main point of contact. Use it as a go-to for all questions relating to your activity, sales, performance, any particular order, or any other Back Market services.
Learn how to contact us

 

 

Manage your Customer Care tab

By clicking on the "Customer Care" tab in your Back Office, you’ll arrive in the after-sales area, which is composed of four main tabs:

 

  • Open tasks
  • All requests

Keep in mind that Back Market will manage all your customer requests and will only ask you to take action when necessary through a notification in the Back Office. 

Learn more about Customer Care by Back Market
 

 

Manage your orders

When it comes to managing your orders, we have plenty of resources. Start with the order page, this is where you’ll find order support and process your orders.
Learn how the orders page works

Please note that you must provide the IMEI of every smartphone sold on Back Market in the Back Office within one working day after shipment. The IMEI must also appear on the invoice.
Learn how to fill in IMEIs

Please note our other guides for managing your orders:

 

  • Manage transport issues
  • Manage return requests
  • Manage full or partial refunds

 


How do I complete this module?

To complete this module, please answer this 3-minute form. If you pass, your sales limitation will automatically increase from 1 to 3 sales per day.

To continue with the next training, click here Quality and Seller Support Center.
